sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Same state. SPACE didn't change anything. The player is at rows 20-23, cols 20-39, and the red block is at rows 16-19, cols 8-31.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe I need to move the player to a different position relative to the merged red block.
In level 1, the player moved to the center (between the red blocks) and pressed SPACE. In level 2, after merging, the red block is at rows 16-19, cols 8-31. The center of this block is around rows 17-18, cols 19-20.
Maybe I need to move the player to overlap with the center of the red block and press SPACE.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the level 1 solution. In level 1, the player moved DOWN 3, RIGHT 3 from the initial position. The initial position was rows 24-27, cols 8-27. After DOWN 3, RIGHT 3, the player was at rows 36-39, cols 20-39.
But in level 2, the initial position is rows 36-39, cols 20-39. This is the same position as the level 1 completion position!
So maybe the level 2 solution is the inverse: move UP 3, LEFT 3 from the initial position. This would put the player at rows 24-27, cols 8-27.
But I already tried this, and it swapped the player with red 2.
Hmm, let me think about this more carefully. In level 1, the player moved to rows 36-39, cols 20-39 and pressed SPACE. This completed the level. In level 2, the player starts at rows 36-39, cols 20-39. Maybe the player needs to move to a different position.
Actually, I just realized something. In level 1, after the player moved to rows 36-39, cols 20-39 and pressed SPACE, the level completed. The player was not adjacent to any red block at that position (the red blocks were at rows 8-11, cols 24-35 and rows 16-19, cols 40-51).
So in level 1, SPACE at a non-adjacent position completed the level. But in level 2, SPACE at the initial position (rows 36-39, cols 20-39) just reset the player.
Wait, that's contradictory. Let me re-check.
Actually, in my earlier exploration, I pressed SPACE at the initial position in level 2, and the player moved back to the initial position. This suggests that SPACE resets the player when not adjacent to a red block.
But in level 1, SPACE at a non-adjacent position completed the level. So the behavior of SPACE is different between levels.
Hmm, maybe I'm misunderstanding the level 1 solution. Let me re-check.
Actually, I don't have the level 1 state anymore. Let me just try different approaches for level 2.
One idea: maybe after merging the red blocks, I need to move the player to a specific position and press SPACE. Let me try moving the player to rows 16-19, cols 8-27 (overlapping with the merged red block) and pressing SPACE.
From rows 20-23, cols 20-39:
- UP 1, LEFT 2: rows 16-19, cols 8-27
This would put the player overlapping with the red block. Let me try this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at rows 20-23, cols 20-39
# Try moving to overlap with merged red block
# UP 1, LEFT 2 → rows 16-19, cols 8-27 (overlaps with red block at rows 16-19, cols 8-31)
action(['UP', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
